issues.file: test: AC-001-1: Meta-banner appears above all content on desktop
test: AC-001-1: Meta-banner appears above all content on desktop
test: AC-001-1: Meta-banner appears above all content on desktop

observations: The meta-banner at the top is present and prominent, correctly positioned above all content with 'view spec' and 'view seed' links visible.

The hero headline 'Build software from intent, not assumption' is clear and readable, though the accent color on 'intent' (purple) gives a subtle SaaS-landing-page feel that leans slightly against the editorial aesthetic described in the spec.

The 'How RootSpec works' four-step grid uses a light sans-serif treatment that reads cleanly, but the numbered step labels (01–04) are very faint gray and may be difficult to read at small viewport sizes.

The hierarchy explorer accordion and 'Try the spec wizard' section are visible and structurally present, though the screenshot resolution makes it hard to confirm interactive affordances (expand/collapse cues) are clearly communicated.
The footer attribution ('Built autonomously by Claude · April 21, 2026') is present but very small and low-contrast against the light background — users who care about the meta-framing may miss it.
